daily_fact: Six years after pandemic shutdowns, Staten Island is celebrating restaurant resilience as longtime neighborhood eateries adapted, survived closures, and continue serving their communities across the borough.
topline_md: New York City faces a broad civic squeeze: major transit and street disruptions, protests and lawsuits over government accountability, discrimination, accessibility and ride-hail enforcement, while housing, health care and aging affordability pressures intensify alongside a few preservation and community wins.

civics_politics.title: Civics & Politics
civics_politics.summary_md:
### Civics & Politics

- NYC sued to block ride-hail app Empower, which could affect access to its cheaper rides if service is halted. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMipgFBVV95cUxPY1EtMjFTNzl0V0UyRk85OUlwWWI0X19OaDJsRnBDS3NGM09meXU4V1Vrb0F0ZnJQZmxSVEhYajJPaHZHVncwU04zMHVrdFB2WHN1NUVrQzJDT1dtSkYwMXRlV090Zy15dTdkMEdvVVBFM2tXV0FfYXJ6QUJMNXZ5U00yTFFQQk0yWmUxR1hLTlZseEFXZ0lEbk9EZzN5R3ZyV25GN1RR?oc=5)
- NYC again denied records on 9/11 toxins, prompting a new lawsuit over public access to health-related information. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMisgFBVV95cUxQSmZYb3RqQ29oWlpMZ25PTkZIaXhRTXZDX3hfeWJVbEV5bW1Ld184bGNFQ3VYcFVuY2t0S2dCd0d1aWJkSDhJWk1zSkE0azd3aG5OYzFiT0hhXzRaTGc3YWJOYlRkZjhBd094X1MwUDZua2xtelVOUHI4VF90cXFlaG9WNWhKRkNQa0hOT0J3Q2ZjY0dlWmJjTUx3bUZmazUtYTM2OGFYZmJ5ZWJOcTRJaEd3?oc=5)
- Lawmakers are urging Gov. Hochul to revive NYC’s free-bus pilot, which could lower commuting costs if approved.
